title: pilosella incana
intro: Pilosella incana, also known as the hoary false fox-glove or hoary mouse-ear, is a perennial plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, making it a potential treatment for colds and flu. Pilosella incana is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
